'''Lobaoc''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Mieru'kar sector/Legends|Mieru'kar sector]]. Its surface featured [[Water/Legends|lakes]] and [[Swamp/Legends|marshes]], which were home to the [[Limb/Legends|tentacled]] and [[Diet/Legends|omnivorous]] [[Dianoga/Legends|dianogas]]. [[Human/Legends|Human]] and [[Near-Human]] [[Farmer/Legends|farmers]] maintained several [[City/Legends|settlements]] on Lobaoc. At some point between [[67 BBY/Legends|67 BBY]] and [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] learned of the existence of a young [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Unidentified Human female (Lobaoc)|girl]] on Lobaoc. The [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] [[Mission to Lobaoc|dispatched]] the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Ambaln]] to [[Jedi investigator/Legends|investigate]] the situation and bring the [[Youngling/Legends|child]] back to the Order for [[Jedi training/Legends|Jedi training]]. Upon realizing that Ambaln had fallen to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] during a prior assignment, the Council also sent a group of individuals to Lobaoc after the fallen Jedi.

On Lobaoc, Ambaln convinced the [[Gender/Legends|girl's]] [[Glirin family|parents]] to give up the child for training, and he subsequently set a trap for any agents of the Jedi Council sent after him. When the group arrived in the planet's [[Koab]] village, Ambaln's [[J9 worker drone/Legends|droid]], [[Z-N0]], led the individuals toward a boathouse, where Ambaln was hiding. At that point, dianogas ambushed the group, and Ambaln used the distraction to attempt to escape to his [[Whitecloak fighter|starship]].

==Description==
Lobaoc was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Wizards" /> located in the [[Lobaoc system/Legends|Lobaoc system]], a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Mieru'kar sector/Legends|Mieru'kar sector]].<ref name="EAOC Lobaoc" /> It was a [[Water/Legends|watery]] world of lakes and [[Swamp/Legends|marshes]], the depths of which were inhabited by [[Dianoga/Legends|dianogas]],<ref name="Wizards" /> [[Limb/Legends|tentacled]] [[Diet/Legends|omnivores]] that were originally [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to<ref name="CSWE Dianoga">{{CSWECite|I|188|dianoga}}</ref> the [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]]<ref name="EAOC Vodran">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Vodran system/Legends|Vodran system]]</ref> planet [[Vodran/Legends|Vodran]] but had spread throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="CSWE Vodran">{{CSWECite|III|310|Vodran}}</ref>

==History==
[[File:Ambaln.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Jedi investigator Ambaln was dispatched on a mission to Lobaoc.]]
At some point between [[67 BBY/Legends|67 BBY]] and [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="Dates">"[[Swim Meet]]" is set in the [[Rise of the Empire era]], which the [[Wookieepedia:The Star Wars Legends Novels Timeline|''Star Wars'' Legends Novels Timeline]] dates to between [[67 BBY/Legends|67 BBY]] and [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]. In addition, the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]]—which, along with the rest of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], was disbanded in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''—is extant during the events of the adventure; therefore, "Swim Meet" must be set at some point between 67 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]] and 19 BBY.</ref> the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] learned of the existence of a young [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Unidentified Human female (Lobaoc)|child]] on Lobaoc, a [[Gender/Legends|girl]] in the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Glirin family]] who had potential for [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] as a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. To appraise the situation and bring the [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|potential Jedi]] back for training, the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] dispatched the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Jedi Consular]] and [[Jedi investigator/Legends|investigator]] [[Ambaln]] to Lobaoc. However, after the investigator had already departed on his [[Mission to Lobaoc|mission]], the Council realized that Ambaln, whose prior assignment had been as a deep undercover operative within a criminal syndicate, had fallen to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. The Mon Calamari had grown disillusioned with the Jedi Council's leadership and planned to create a new council, starting with the [[Youngling/Legends|child]] on Lobaoc. To foil Ambaln's plans and bring him back to the Jedi Order for rehabilitation, the Council then also sent a group of individuals to Lobaoc.<ref name="Wizards" />

After arriving at the [[Koab]] [[City/Legends|village]] on Lobaoc, Ambaln flew his [[Whitecloak fighter]] out to the Glirin family's [[Farm/Legends|farm]] in the marsh outside the settlement. After convincing the Glirin girl's [[Parent/Legends|parents]] to give up their child for Jedi training, the Mon Calamari dispatched his [[Translator droid/Legends|translator]] and personal servant, the [[J9 worker drone/Legends|J9 worker drone]] [[Z-N0]], to walk back to Koab. The [[Droid/Legends|droid]] was ordered to wait by the village's landing pads for the arrival of anyone looking for Ambaln and then to lead them to a boathouse, where the fallen Jedi claimed he would talk to the Jedi Order's agents sent after him. Leaving the Glirin girl to wait at his [[Starfighter/Legends|starship]] about a mile from Koab, Ambaln himself then went to wait at the boathouse. He intended to spring a trap upon the Jedi Council's agents by having a group of dianogas ambush them on the causeway connecting the structure to the rest of the village.<ref name="Wizards" />

{{RPG|"[[Swim Meet]]"}}
Early in the following morning, the group sent by the Jedi Council after Ambaln arrived in Koab. While acquiring information from the locals, the individuals learned that Z-N0 had come back from the Glirin farm mid-morning that [[Standard day/Legends|day]]. The group then met the droid, who led it to the boathouse in which Ambaln was hiding. At that point, the dianogas attacked the Council's agents, and the Mon Calamari attempted to use the distraction to swim to his [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. Although the group sent by the Council caught up with the fallen Jedi before he was able to escape, Ambaln claimed that he had hidden the girl in the planet's marshes and that she would [[Death/Legends|die]] before the agents could find her. The Mon Calamari then distracted the group enough to once again escape toward his ship, [[Animal bond|enlisting]] the aid of more dianogas to ward off his pursuers.<ref name="Wizards" />
{{Endgame}}

==Inhabitants==
Lobaoc featured a number of settlements inhabited by Human and [[Near-Human]] [[Farmer/Legends|farmers]] who harvested water-grown [[Plant/Legends|crops]] in Lobaoc's murky shallows. The Human Glirin family kept a farm on Lobaoc. When the Jedi Knight Ambaln visited Koab, his mechanical servant, Z-N0, was the only droid in the settlement and thereby was noticed and remembered by most of the village's inhabitants.<ref name="Wizards" />

==Locations==
Koab was a village on Lobaoc that had a population of a few thousand individuals. Floating causeways connected the center of Koab to warehouses and storage sheds located at the settlement's edges, as well as to a collection of landing pads—large metal slabs floating above the ground on [[repulsorlift pod]]s—located at several flat, mostly dry areas that constituted the village's [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]]. The longest causeway of Koab led to a boathouse.<ref name="Wizards" />

==Behind the scenes==
Lobaoc was introduced in "[[Swim Meet]]," a ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' mini-adventure authored by [[Jeff Quick]] and published on the [[Wizards of the Coast]] [[Wizards.com|website]] on [[August 22]], [[2002]].<ref name="Wizards" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Lobaoc [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet Lobaoc, in grid square N-3.<ref name="TEA" />

"Swim Meet" presents several options for how the player characters' encounter with Ambaln on Lobaoc may proceed. If Ambaln is caught during his initial escape attempt, the Mon Calamari distracts the player characters with conversation before attempting to flee once again. If at that point Ambaln loses his pursuers, he swims back to his starship and leaves Lobaoc. The fallen Jedi then spends a few [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] staying hidden before returning to Lobaoc with the young girl to begin her training in his own doctrine of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. This article assumes the scenario plays out as described.<ref name="Wizards" />

Although "Swim Meet" is set in the [[Rise of the Empire era]], the adventure can also be adapted for play in the [[Rebellion era]] or the [[New Jedi Order era]]. In the former case, instead of Ambaln being a Jedi, the adventure suggests that the Mon Calamari operates a child [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] ring and employs additional confederates instead of calling on dianogas for assistance. If the adventure is instead set in the [[New Jedi Order]] era, gamemaster players only need to change all references to the Jedi Council to refer to the [[Jedi Praxeum|Jedi Academy]]. In addition, if one of the player characters is of the [[Fringer/Legends|fringer]] class, the adventure suggests that Lobaoc, or a world nearby, could be the character's homeworld.<ref name="Wizards" />
